mnemonics.title: Event Handling Steps
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'R-R-H-E': 1) Register event listener, 2) Runtime system monitors, 3) Handler identified, 4) Event dispatched to handler execution.

mnemonics.title: Event Propagation Phases
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'C-T-B': Capture → Target → Bubble. Events travel down (capture), hit target, then travel up (bubble).

mnemonics.title: Event Properties Remembered by 'SOFA'
mnemonics.content: Source (where event occurred), Object (target element), Function (handler), Arguments (event data).

acronyms.usage: Applications with visual components like buttons, menus that generate events
Programming interface for web documents where events are attached to elements
Architecture pattern where Controller handles user events
Set of tools for building event-driven applications
Platform where user interactions generate events

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular conveyor belt (event queue) with a worker (event loop) continuously picking up items (events) and delivering them to appropriate stations (handlers).
Think of bubbles rising from depth to surface - an event starts at the deepest element and 'bubbles' up through parent elements.
Imagine a net being lowered from the top of a tree (root) to catch a bird (target element) - the event travels downward through ancestors.
Think of a callback as a callback phone number you leave - it gets called back when the event (situation) occurs.
A delegate is like a post office - it knows where to deliver your message (event) to the correct recipient (handler method).

analogies.analogy: Like a security guard responding to door alarms, camera motion, or visitor buzzers - the guard's actions depend entirely on which alarm triggers.
Like a restaurant's order queue - orders (events) are taken in the order received and prepared (handled) one at a time by the kitchen.
Like a subscription to a newspaper - you get notified (callback) whenever a new edition (event) is published.
Like dropping a stone in water - ripples (events) start at the impact point and spread outward (bubble) or inward (capture) depending on perspective.
Like a phone with voicemail - you don't have to answer immediately (non-blocking), you check messages (handlers) when convenient.
